stem: A bubble sort is being used to sort the list [5, 3, 8, 1] into ascending order.
model_answer: Compare the first two items (5 and 3); because 5 > 3, swap them, giving [3, 5, 8, 1]. Compare the next pair (5 and 8); leave in place. Compare the final pair (8 and 1); swap, giving [3, 5, 1, 8]. That is the end of the first pass. Repeat passes, one element shorter each time, until a full pass completes with no swaps — the list is then sorted.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Look for: pairwise comparison, swap when out of order, largest value reaches the end after each pass, terminate when a pass makes no swaps.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe, using numbered steps, how a bubble sort algorithm would sort the list [5, 3, 8, 1] into ascending order. Use the list's values to illustrate each pass.

mark_points.text: compares adjacent pairs of items starting at the beginning of the list
swaps a pair when the left item is greater than the right item
after each pass the largest remaining value settles at the right-hand end
repeats passes until a whole pass makes no swaps (list is sorted)

misconceptions.label: Single pass sorts the list
misconceptions.description: One pass of bubble sort only guarantees the largest item reaches the end; further passes are needed for full ordering.
